message.content: Adversaries may exploit Windows File and Directory Permissions Modification to bypass access control lists (ACLs) and gain unauthorized access to protected files. This technique involves altering Discretionary Access Control Lists (DACLs) using built-in Windows commands like `icacls`, `cacls`, `takeown`, and `attrib`, or PowerShell cmdlets. By modifying DACLs, adversaries can escalate privileges, establish persistence, or disable security tools. Common exploitation methods include resetting permissions, taking ownership of files, and granting full access to all users. Notable malware such as BitPaymer, CaddyWiper, Grandoreiro, JPIN, Ryuk, WannaCry, WastedLocker, and Wizard Spider have utilized these techniques to further their objectives. Mitigation strategies include regularly auditing file and directory permissions, employing principle of least privilege, and using security monitoring tools to detect unauthorized changes to ACLs.
